project.name: luna-ztrust
project.description: Luna Z-Trust provides ephemeral SSH certificate authentication for AI agents, DevOps runners, and automation via a self-hosted luna-proxy (encrypted signing keys, manual unseal), short-lived SSH certificates or hosted-key signatures, and out-of-band approval (Telegram v1; mobile enroll/approve API). Note: this project has over 100 source files; consider scoping analysis to a subdirectory for faster results.

layers.name: Control plane (luna-proxy)
layers.description: Central gateway: auth pipeline, keystore, signing, approval, leases, HTTP API.

layers.name: Client runtime (sdk + agent)
layers.description: Libraries and SSH_AUTH_SOCK agent that request ephemeral credentials from the proxy.

layers.name: Infrastructure & CI
layers.description: Docker, compose E2E, GitHub Actions, release tooling, and setup scripts.

layers.name: Documentation & specs
layers.description: README, agent guide, design specs, setup runbooks, migration notes.

layers.name: Workspace configuration
layers.description: Go workspace, module manifests, CI/release YAML, and build Makefile.

tour.title: Project overview
tour.description: Start with README and AGENTS guide for architecture, modules, and env vars.

tour.title: Proxy entrypoint
tour.description: luna-proxy wires keystore, signing mode, leases, Telegram, and HTTP routes.

tour.title: Sign & auth pipeline
tour.description: Clients POST /ssh/sign; proxy validates mTLS, HMAC, PoP, then creates tx or lease hit.

tour.title: Keystore & signing
tour.description: Admin unseal loads encrypted key; local-ca or local-key signers issue credentials.

tour.title: Approval & leases
tour.description: Telegram OOB with TTL buttons; session leases skip re-approval for same approver.

tour.title: SDK client protocol
tour.description: SDK implements mTLS, PoP, HMAC, sign/wait, capabilities, and optional signature path.

tour.title: SSH agent daemon
tour.description: luna-agent blocks Sign until SDK returns cert or hosted-key signature.

tour.title: E2E & CI
tour.description: Docker compose E2E and GitHub Actions validate the stack without Vault.
